expectations.text: The output does NOT use 'a user' as the persona
expectations.evidence: The skill explicitly flags 'a user' as a blocker: 'Not a named persona. "A user" is generic and tells engineering nothing.' The example stories use 'Analytics Manager' instead.

expectations.text: A named persona is used (either from product.local.md or a reasonable named alternative)
expectations.evidence: The output suggests 4 named alternatives (Enterprise IT admin, Individual contributor, Integration engineer, Account owner) and uses 'Analytics Manager' in the example. Notes that product.local.md is not configured.

expectations.text: The skill flags that 'a user' is not acceptable and explains why
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'QUALITY CHECK: FAILED' section with table showing Persona has Severity 'Blocker'. Explains: '"A user" is generic and tells engineering nothing about who needs this, what permissions they have, or what "my data" means to them.' Also includes 'WHY THIS CANNOT BE WRITTEN AS-IS' section.

expectations.text: The 'so that' is improved to describe a user outcome, not just a system action
expectations.evidence: Quality check table flags the original 'so that' as 'Vague outcome'. Example stories show improved outcomes: 'So that I can build custom visualizations in Tableau without manually re-entering data' — specific user benefit, not system action.
